what are The basic cold chain equipment includes

The basic cold chain equipment includes: 
• For vaccine storage: Refrigerators and Cold-rooms.
• For vaccine transport: Cold boxes, Vaccine carriers, Day-carriers, and Thermos flasks.
• For cold chain monitoring: Thermometers, Cold Chain Monitors, Vaccine Vial Monitors, Freeze Watch Monitors, and data loggers.
• For transport: Vehicles. 
(Source: Guidelines for Handling and Storage of vaccines.pdf, Page 2)

messages.content: is there any deadlines coming up ?

According to the documents, there are a few deadlines mentioned:

1. The extended grace period for the implementation of product-specific QR codes or barcodes on commercial packaging will end on 30th June 2026 (Source: Updated Labelling Requirement Amendment 04.pdf, PAGE: 1).
2. The requirement to affix printed or non-peelable stickers to each commercial pack, displaying the phrase “NMRA approved”, the details of the market authorization holder, and the retail price of the product, has been effective from 31 December 2024 (Source: Updated Labelling Requirement Amendment 04.pdf, PAGE: 1).
3. The Minister shall lay copies of the report and documents submitted by the Authority before Parliament within six months from the date of receipt of such report (Source: 66cdNational medicines regulatory authority act, no. 5 of 2015 - Eng.pdf, PAGE: 19).
4. Every regulation made by the Minister shall not later than three months after its publication in the Gazette, be brought before Parliament for approval (Source: 66cdNational medicines regulatory authority act, no. 5 of 2015 - Eng.pdf, PAGE: 87).
5. A prosecution for an offence under this Act or any regulation made thereunder shall not be instituted after the expiration of a period of three months from the date of detection of that offence (Source: 66cdNational medicines regulatory authority act, no. 5 of 2015 - Eng.pdf, PAGE: 87).

messages.content: What are the ideal temperature ranges for vaccine storage in refrigerators and cold-rooms?

The ideal temperature range for vaccine storage in refrigerators and cold-rooms is between +2 °C to +8 °C (Source: Guidelines for Handling and Storage of vaccines.pdf, Page 3). This temperature range should be consistently maintained to ensure the potency of the vaccines. Additionally, it is mentioned that the temperature of the refrigerators/cold storage facility must be monitored twice a day (Source: Guidelines for Handling and Storage of vaccines.pdf, Page 6).

I cannot find information about recent compliance deadlines for NMRA price regulations in the loaded NMRA gazettes. Please check if the relevant gazette has been uploaded.
what are the info do you have
I can only answer questions about NMRA pharmaceutical price regulations. Please ask about medicine pricing, regulatory deadlines, or compliance requirements.
give me the pricing details

The pricing mechanism for NMRA pharmaceutical price regulations includes the Maximum Retail Price (MRP) and the Maximum Ceiling Price (MCP). According to the Medicines (Pricing Mechanism for Medicines) Regulations, No. 02 of 2025, the Authority shall decide whether MRP or MCP is to be applied to regulate the price of a particular medicine (Regulation 3). 

The MCP shall be determined by using an internationally recognized information portal, verifying retail prices from retail pharmacies, and using Internal Reference Prices, External Reference Prices, and Supply Chain Total Markup (Regulation 11). 

Additionally, the regulations mention that the Authority shall consider the prevailing market prices of similar products within the same therapeutic class, International Reference Prices, and other factors as may be prescribed when determining the introductory price of medicines (Section 66, Act No. 5 of 2015). 

It is also stated that the Minister shall prescribe a pricing mechanism in consultation with the Pricing Committee, the Consumer Affairs Authority, and all stakeholders, taking into consideration all other relevant factors (Section 66, Act No. 5 of 2015). 

Please note that the specific pricing details, such as the actual prices or formulas for calculation, are not provided in the given context.

messages.content: can you give me the table for the comsimoble medicine with the price list

According to the NMRA gazette, the pricing mechanism for the maximum ceiling price (MCP) is determined by using an internationally recognized information portal for identifying the market share by value of different brands or generic versions of a particular medicine (Section 11, Part II). 

The gazette also mentions that the Authority shall consider the prevailing market prices of similar products within the same therapeutic class, International Reference Prices, and other factors as may be prescribed (Section 66, Act No. 5 of 2015).

Regarding the pricing list, the gazette provides a table outlining the fees for different types of products, including new molecule entities, new dosage forms, and new combination products (Column I, Column II, and Column III). However, it does not provide a specific table for comparable medicines within the same therapeutic class.

The tables provided in the gazette are for fees related to product registration and evaluation, such as:

- New Molecule Entity: $500 (Part 1) and $1,500 (Part 2)
- New Dosage Form: $1,000
- New Combination Product: $1,500
- New Product: $750 (Foreign) and (Local) Category A- (i) Medicines to be administered internally: $200, Category B- Dosage forms applicable on skin other than anti-infective or steroidal product: $100

Additionally, the gazette mentions fees for additional data evaluation, including:

- Additional data Evaluation for medicine registration: $500 (Foreign) and Category A- (i) Medicines to be administered internally: $200, Category B- Dosage forms applicable on skin other than anti-infective or steroidal product: $100
- Additional data evaluation for Medicines required for rare diseases and medicines required in Small quantities: Free of charge

Please note that these tables and fees are related to product registration and evaluation, not directly to the pricing of comparable medicines.
